notes:
cao thủ = a master, a highly skilled person at something. It’s used for someone who’s really good, often at games, martial arts, or a specific skill.

It’s a countable noun: you can say một cao thủ. Often followed by the field or skill to show what they’re good at.  

It has a slightly dramatic or impressive tone.  
Close synonyms: bậc thầy (more formal, “great master”), chuyên gia (expert, more professional).  
Opposite vibe: tay mơ (newbie, amateur).
exampleSentencesTranslated: He is a master in the city's chess community.
She wants to become a master of English to study abroad.
If you practice hard, you can also become a swimming master.
exampleSentences: Anh ấy là một cao thủ trong làng cờ vua của thành phố.
Chị muốn trở thành cao thủ tiếng Anh để đi du học.
Nếu em luyện tập chăm chỉ, em cũng có thể trở thành cao thủ bơi lội.

It has a slightly dramatic or impressive tone.  
Close synonyms: **bậc thầy** (more formal, “great master”), **chuyên gia** (expert, more professional).  
Opposite vibe: **tay mơ** (newbie, amateur).
